commands.repair_reason: Human maintainer review is required because the PR has the protected maintainer label and no narrow ClawSweeper repair is needed.; Cleared: The diff adds no dependencies, permissions, or secret handling; the only network behavior change is one retry after a provider error with opaque encrypted reasoning blobs removed. (sha=809e5ead3f36e33591ff7fc4cbeb8e0417682e03)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a one-time OpenAI Responses retry that strips `encrypted_content` fields from replay input after `invalid_encrypted_content`, updates regression coverage, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body includes a before Crabbox run reproducing the injected stale `encrypted_content` failure on live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and current main source shows those JSON `thinkingSignature` blocks are replayed as Responses reasoning items.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is required because the PR has the protected `maintainer` label and no narrow ClawSweeper repair is needed.

target.title: fix(agents): recover stale Codex encrypted reasoning replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: stale mirrored Codex Responses reasoning signatures can carry provider-encrypted `encrypted_content` into later replay requests and fail with `invalid_encrypted_content`.
- Solution: retry failed OpenAI/Codex Responses stream creation once after stripping only `encrypted_content` fields from the replay payload.
- What changed: the OpenAI Responses transport now preserves normal encrypted reasoning replay on the first attempt, then performs a targeted sanitized retry only after the provider reports `invalid_encrypted_content`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Telegram-specific behavior, channel fallback UX, compaction auth selection, or generic transcript rewriting is changed.

## Motivation

- Prevent real channel-bound Codex sessions from silently failing after stale encrypted reasoning history poisons later OpenAI/Codex Responses replay.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83836
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` turns fail with `400 invalid_encrypted_content` when replay history contains stale mirrored `thinkingSignature.encrypted_content`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_a155e5ab36ef`, run `run_43bba1e91cde`, current branch checkout with dirty fix synced, live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through PI runtime using an isolated OAuth profile state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: full-resync Crabbox script created an isolated OpenClaw state, ran one live Codex turn, appended a parent-linked transcript entry containing stale `encrypted_content`, then ran a second live Codex turn on the same session.

Evidence after fix: second turn output included `[openai-transport] [responses] retrying without encrypted reasoning content provider=openai-codex api=openai-codex-responses model=gpt-5.5`, returned `SECOND_FIX_OK`, and printed `FIX_PROOF_RECOVERED_FROM_INVALID_ENCRYPTED_CONTENT=true`.

Observed result after fix: the poisoned replay no longer terminates the turn; OpenClaw retries with encrypted replay content removed and completes the live Codex response through `agentHarnessId: pi`.

What was not tested: Telegram delivery UX, channel-visible fallback messaging, and the separate compaction/auth-profile concern from the issue thread.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): AWS Crabbox run `run_ca2af4a2e5ad`, lease `cbx_712f8d43b376`, reproduced the same injected stale encrypted replay with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; second turn failed with `code=invalid_encrypted_content` and printed `REPRODUCED_CODEX_INVALID_ENCRYPTED_CONTENT=true`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `convertResponsesMessages` replays JSON `thinkingSignature` blocks as Responses `reasoning` input items, including provider-owned `encrypted_content` that can become invalid outside its original request/thread/auth context.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the transport treated `invalid_encrypted_content` as a terminal model failure instead of a recoverable stale replay sanitization failure.
- Contributing context (if known): channel-bound Codex sessions can persist mirrored thinking signatures across turns, so one-time transcript cleanup is insufficient after later successful Codex turns write fresh encrypted reasoning again.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: sanitized retry payloads strip nested `encrypted_content` without changing reasoning IDs, message IDs, function call IDs, or tool pairing.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in the shared OpenAI/Codex Responses transport request boundary, below Telegram and mirrored-history callsites.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing native and custom Codex-compatible Responses replay tests cover ID preservation and now explicitly preserve normal encrypted reasoning replay.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex/OpenAI Responses turns that fail only because stale replayed encrypted reasoning cannot be verified now recover once instead of ending without a deliverable assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[session replay with stale encrypted_content] -> [Responses 400 invalid_encrypted_content] -> [turn fails]

After:
[session replay with stale encrypted_content] -> [Responses 400 invalid_encrypted_content] -> [retry without encrypted_content] -> [reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: one additional Responses retry can occur after the provider returns `invalid_encrypted_content`; the retry removes opaque encrypted reasoning blobs and preserves visible replay/tool structure.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: AWS Crabbox Linux
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm checkout via Crabbox full-resync
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): direct local agent session reproducing the shared channel replay failure below Telegram
- Relevant config (redacted): isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` with `openai-codex` OAuth auth profile and PI runtime forced for `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`

### Steps

1. Run a live Codex turn in a fresh session and verify `FIRST_FIX_OK`.
2. Append a parent-linked assistant transcript entry with JSON `thinkingSignature` containing stale `encrypted_content`.
3. Run a second live Codex turn in the same session and verify retry plus `SECOND_FIX_OK`.

### Expected

- The stale encrypted replay is recovered by a retry that strips `encrypted_content`.

### Actual

- The live proof recovered and printed `FIX_PROOF_RECOVERED_FROM_INVALID_ENCRYPTED_CONTENT=true`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: focused Vitest coverage, `$autoreview`, live AWS Crabbox injected stale encrypted replay proof.
- Edge cases checked: normal first-attempt encrypted reasoning replay remains preserved; retry stripping preserves IDs/tool-call structure; retry is gated to `invalid_encrypted_content`.
- What you did **not** verify: Telegram channel fallback UX and compaction auth-profile behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: stripping encrypted reasoning too broadly could reduce valid Responses replay quality.
  - Mitigation: first-attempt replay is unchanged; stripping happens only on one retry after `invalid_encrypted_content`.
target.author: joshavant
